svg.text: THE MAGIC FUNNEL
Instructions
What You Need
· special double-walled funnel with finger-controlled holes
How to Play
Effect: Water flows freely from a funnel, then stops completely at the
performer's command — and flows again on command. Method: the funnel has a
hidden air tube running through its interior with a small hole at the top.
Covering the hole with a fingertip creates a partial vacuum that holds the
water suspended — the same principle as pressing your finger on a straw to
hold liquid inside. Remove the finger and water flows freely. The finger is
hidden on the back of the handle. This looks completely miraculous. Make a
simplified version with a bendy straw to demonstrate the principle.
Did You Know?
The Magic Funnel demonstrates atmospheric pressure — the same principle
prevents liquid from flowing from a covered test tube. Torricelli discovered
the principle in 1644 as he invented the barometer, but Victorian conjurers
exploited it for entertainment long before physics teachers used it for
education.
Original Victorian Description
Water pours freely from a funnel, then stops entirely at the performer's
command — then pours again. A double-walled funnel has small holes in its
inner tube; when the performer secretly covers one hole with a finger, air is
excluded and water cannot flow. Uncovering the hole allows flow to resume.
Source: The Magician's Own Book (1862)
Heritage Parlor · Page 2 of 2
